comments.author: @GlobalRealLifeDocumentary
comments.text: Thailand isn't just about beautiful beaches—it's a country filled with incredible temples, world-famous food, vibrant culture, and some of the friendliest people you'll ever meet. 🇹🇭 Here's my question: if you could spend just one week in Thailand, would you explore the islands, Bangkok, or the northern mountains? Tell us why!

comments.author: @aibyjongray
comments.text: You seem to know so much about Thailand, but can't even pronounce 'Pattaya' correctly.  It is 'Pat-a-ya'. If you could see it written in Thai you can't get it wrong. It was the early German residents who used the Philippine pronunciation of ' ay' to be 'eye'. These, like many settlers  never bothered to learn the language, and the Thais had to tolerate 'falang thai', which is often wrong. And there is no comparison between restaurant food and street food. Street food is meant to feed poor Thais and mostly consists of dried fish, fish balls and skewered meat...nice as a novelty,  but daily, no thanks.

comments.author: @CountryTravelDocumentary
comments.text:
Thailand is truly a country full of beauty, culture, and unforgettable experiences! 🇹🇭✨ From ancient temples and colorful traditions to amazing food, tropical islands, and the warmth of its people, Thailand has a special energy that attracts the world. This documentary beautifully captures the hidden stories of this incredible country. 🌏🔥
What do you love most about Thailand — the culture, beaches, food, history, or lifestyle? 👇 Let’s discuss!
